feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Temple Maker 64
entry.content: Have you made any dungeons in Temple Maker 64? any opinions? It's like mario maker but for Zelda Ocarina of Time dungeons, you make dungeons and share them with people, there's a demo on Steam and a Kickstarter that is not doing too well. Vinesauce played it the other day: https://youtu.be/J05ZGfV9cFk?si=rPo -TImrnT-AOqJe

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Do you think cloud-gaming is the future? You no longer have to keep up with the …
entry.content: Do you think cloud-gaming is the future? You no longer have to keep up with the latest hardware (so it could potentially be a lot cheaper in the long run), and you can run everything at max settings from any old device you have (since you're only streaming the game instead of emulating it on your device).

entry.title: /int/ International: There was a merchant in Baghdad who sent his servant to market to buy provisions
entry.content: and in a little while the servant came back, white and trembling, and said, Master, just now when I was in the marketplace I was jostled by a woman in the crowd and when I turned I saw it was Death that had jostled me. She looked at me and made a threatening gesture; now, lend me your horse, and I will ride away from this city and avoid my fate. I will go to Samarra and there Death will not find me. The merchant lent him his horse, and the servant mounted it, and he dug his spurs in its flanks a

entry.title: /b/ Random: Retard Steve, as he was known to the locals, lived a life that was not defined b…
entry.content: Retard Steve, as he was known to the locals, lived a life that was not defined by his challenges, but by his unwavering resilience. He was a gay man with AIDS, and his mental capacity was not like that of others. Despite these obstacles, Steve found joy in the simplest of things - a warm sun on his face, the sound of birds chirping, or the taste of his favorite ice cream. Steve lived in a small group home on the outskirts of town, where he was cared for by a team of dedicated caregivers. They he

entry.title: /int/ International: One thing I will never get about Indians is the way they prepare their dishes. W…
entry.content: One thing I will never get about Indians is the way they prepare their dishes. Whats the point of adding 20 different spices to every dish if you're just gonna dump an ungodly amount of chili into it so you cannot taste anything but the chili?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: browser seems to keep only a relatively small amount of history
entry.content: The topic is pretty niche, so please at least bump the thread so it doesn’t sink too quickly. It’s niche, but it’s something that would be useful to every one of us. Right now, my browser seems to keep only a relatively small amount of history and may even delete it after some time. The only thing I want from life is a browser that won’t delete my browsing history and that will work on both phone and PC. Right now I use Waterfox and IceRaven. It also has to support fucking uBlock. Do you have an

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I'm still fucking mad. It's been 13 fucking years, and I'm still mad. I'm so mad…
entry.content: I'm still fucking mad. It's been 13 fucking years, and I'm still mad. I'm so mad, I wish they never made Thieves in Time at all. The worst part about that fucking game is that Sanzaru got the core gameplay and level design completely right, so you keep being pulled through the shitty ancestor gimmicks, minigames and truly dogshit story that couldn't be any worse if it were deliberate ragebait. If I must make an obligatory food analogy, I'd say it's like having a really nice chicken salad sandwic

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Modder-friendly Minecraft remake made in C
entry.content: Hello /g/, I am making a modder-friendly Minecraft remake in C. I am designing the engine to be modders-first. The core game without any lua is extremely barebones and unplayable. What you see in pic rel is a result of the lua modding API. I want the modding API to be on par with GMod's modding API. I will implement features like asset loading into the engine to make modding incredibly powerful alongside creating and bundling incredibly useful libraries to make loading assets from other engines

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: the worst punishment you can give a living creature is force it to endure random…
entry.content: the worst punishment you can give a living creature is force it to endure random signals and noise, it feels like a shock or pain beyond what anything can really describe, its something that invades you, makes you feel guilty, breaks your spirit and after its over, you wonder if you're just gonna experience it again forever when you die, and it makes you want to kill yourself just to get it over with

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >AI won't replace you. AI will supercharge your life. lol no The minute AI kills…
entry.content: >AI won't replace you. AI will supercharge your life. lol no The minute AI kills your labor value, you are stuck with the capital you have. Whatever capital that is, that is what you have until you die. Nothing more and nothing less.

entry.content: thinking of getting a new provider; don't like starlink. entry.title: /mu/ Music: >music theory ruins enjoyment This is such an adorable brainlet cope. I always y…
entry.content: >music theory ruins enjoyment This is such an adorable brainlet cope. I always you see guys circlejerking it on here. First of all it's kind of weak to cling to enjoyment. There is plently in the world to enjoy, and the best sources of musical inspiration are always extramusical. Oftentimes learning music theory can disrupt enjoyment, but there's nothing to fear. You are simply being challenged to use that knowledge to feel music on a deeper level. You will get to a point where you consciously f

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: So now watch what happens. William Herschel discovers planet Uranus by accident.…
entry.content: So now watch what happens. William Herschel discovers planet Uranus by accident. This is after Newton's laws of gravity are published. So now we're tracking the orbit of Uranus. This is not following Newton's law. It's got some deviation. So maybe we found the distance limit over which Newton's laws would apply. That'd be interesting. But other people said Newton was so badass it must work, okay, even there. So instead of suggesting that his law was failing, clever people said maybe his law appl

entry.content: Why do niggers tongue my anus?

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Let's imagine that, in our galaxy of 400 billion suns, there's just us that thin…
entry.content: Let's imagine that, in our galaxy of 400 billion suns, there's just us that thinks, right? I mean, microbes all over the place, but in terms of things that think and can feel and, in a very real sense, bring meaning to the universe. All these things we've talked about, the beauty of these galaxies, they're not beautiful if there's nothing there to perceive them, right? They're just galaxies. So if that's it in this galaxy, then the decisions that we make now as a civilization have galactic impli
